original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.exact_document_title: Hyperparathyroidism (primary): diagnosis, assessment and initial management
original_registry_entry.population: People with features or laboratory findings that may indicate primary hyperparathyroidism and people with confirmed disease.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: Primary and secondary care diagnostic testing, assessment, referral, management and monitoring.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Partial for a generic calcium-result review because the source is disease-specific. Exact only for selected symptom, albumin-adjusted calcium, PTH, renal, vitamin D, DXA and clinician-led follow-up documentation.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.10 — albumin-adjusted calcium and PTH testing
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports thirst, polyuria, constipation, osteoporosis, fragility-fracture and renal-stone context; repeat albumin-adjusted calcium, concurrent PTH and specialist-advice documentation when clinician-selected.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.3 — secondary-care testing and assessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed vitamin D, urine calcium, renal function, DXA and renal-tract imaging in a confirmed or probable primary-hyperparathyroidism context.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.4.10–1.6.2 and monitoring table — follow-up and monitoring
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-arranged calcium, PTH, renal-function, DXA and specialist monitoring with recommendation-specific qualifiers; no interval is auto-generated.

title: Hyperparathyroidism (primary): diagnosis, assessment and initial management
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
population: People with features or laboratory findings that may indicate primary hyperparathyroidism and people with confirmed disease.
intended_setting: Primary and secondary care diagnostic testing, assessment, referral, management and monitoring.
